sanskrit: अधिभूतं क्षरो भावः पुरुषश्चाधिदैवतम् । अधियज्ञोऽहमेवात्र देहे देहभृतां वर ॥
hindiTranslation: हे देहधारियोंमें श्रेष्ठ अर्जुन  क्षरभाव अर्थात् नाशवान् पदार्थको अधिभूत कहते हैं, पुरुष अर्थात् हिरण्यगर्भ ब्रह्माजी अधिदैव हैं और इस देहमें अन्तर्यामीरूपसे मैं ही अधियज्ञ हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: The perishable world is the lower realm; the cosmic person is the radiant being. I am the inner witness of sacrifice in this body, Arjuna.
essence: The changing world, the cosmic order, and the inner witness are not separate.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: The changing world is called the lower realm. The cosmic person is called the radiant being. In this body, Krishna says he himself is the inner witness of sacrifice.
simpleInsight: What seems separate is ordered from within, and the deepest meaning of action is already present.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna asks Krishna to explain the terms needed for dying with awareness. Krishna answers step by step: the changing world, the cosmic presiding being, and finally himself as the inner witness within every body. The teaching builds toward Krishna's guidance at the moment of death.
modernRelevance: You are trying to understand what really matters before a hard goodbye, a hospital room, or a final decision. The mind wants labels; the teaching points to the deeper order behind them.
translationLiteral: अधिभूतं (the lower realm of embodied existence) / क्षरः (perishable) / भावः (state of being) / पुरुषः (cosmic person) / च (and) / अधिदैवतम् (the radiant being of the gods) / अधियज्ञः (inner witness of sacrifice) / अहम् (I) / एव (indeed) / अत्र (here) / देहे (in the body) / देहभृताम् (of embodied beings) / वर (O best)
insight: It shifts attention from outer labels to the hidden presence that makes action meaningful.
